creator: diizzy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Upstream's meson script claims that it's optional but that seems incorrect looking at the code.
https://github.com/audacious-media-player/audacious-plugins/blob/master/src/cdaudio/meson.build#L3

The plan was/is to unbundle all (remaining) consumers, most have been patched already or deprecated while a few are in progress.

CDDB has been on life support for years, there's also no way (or I should probably say sane way) to submit any data because of its current status. As much as I'd like to say it's no hassle we haven't updated consumers with a working server since the previous discontinuation of "the" service which leads me to believe that no one really cares or uses it.

Since there are (good) alternatives which have been adopted by several projects I'd almost place cddb into the "bitrot" category by now given the situation. Projects still clining on to cddb and redirecting to a non working service is probably a good inducation that upstream likely don't use or test functionality.

While we can probably keep it building for quite some time it adds our growing pile of stuff that no one really cares about and just eats up time and resources.

I'll try to make a patch that disables the functionality in a user friendly way.

Best regardsm
Daniel
